text:
Artificial intelligence (AI) systems are becoming deeply integrated into nearly every aspect of modern life. While these technologies offer immense potential, their unchecked deployment poses significant risks to individual rights and broader societal interests. In response, regulators worldwide are introducing various normative frameworks; however, most have relied on non-binding ethical guidelines. Breaking from this trend, the European Union (EU) has established a definitive legal standard with the adoption of the Artificial Intelligence Act in the spring of 2024. 

Formally entering into force on August 1, 2024, the Act introduces a comprehensive, risk-based regulatory structure that applies across all sectors rather than focusing on narrow use cases. As a first-of-its-kind systematic regulation, it mandates binding requirements for high-risk applications while prohibiting practices deemed to pose an unacceptable threat to fundamental rights. Given its broad extraterritorial reach and status as a global trendsetter, the AI Act is set to fundamentally redefine the development and adoption of AI technologies both within Europe and across the international stage.
